models.description: Event-based RSTO (ERST) MILP for real-time joint rolling-stock and timetable rescheduling in an urban rail line (Beijing metro line 1). Termed 'MIP' in the paper. Built over an event-activity network where each event is a train arrival/departure at a station. Decides which events to cancel, the (possibly delayed) realized time of each event, how many rolling-stock units dwell at each station, and depot in/out turnaround connections, minimizing weighted cancellation plus delay cost. Solved directly with CPLEX default parameters via EventBasedFormulation().

sets_indices.name: E
sets_indices.meaning: set of events (each a train arrival at a station in the event-activity network)

sets_indices.name: P
sets_indices.meaning: set of event pairs / consecutive events used for headway and precedence relations

sets_indices.name: I
sets_indices.meaning: set of stations (23 stations of Beijing metro line 1; indices doubled for up/down direction)

sets_indices.name: D
sets_indices.meaning: set of depots (2: indexed i in {0,1})

sets_indices.name: E^{in}_d
sets_indices.meaning: in-events: events arriving at depot d (trains entering service from depot)

sets_indices.name: E^{out}_d
sets_indices.meaning: out-events: events departing toward depot d (trains leaving service to depot)

parameters.name: c^{delay}
parameters.meaning: penalty cost per unit of event delay

parameters.name: a_e
parameters.meaning: planned (scheduled) arrival time of event e

parameters.name: M^{dur}
parameters.meaning: big-M = time span of the event set (last minus first planned arrival time), used to deactivate the realized-time bound for cancelled events

parameters.name: M^{big}
parameters.meaning: large big-M constant used in the depot turnaround timing constraint

parameters.name: epsilon
parameters.meaning: slack travel time / allowed segment running-time variation per section, divided by time unit

parameters.name: T^{turn}
parameters.meaning: minimum turnaround time at a depot/turnback station

parameters.name: n_i
parameters.meaning: number of initially-positioned trains that must be present/covered at station i (count from TrainPosition matching station i or its mirrored down-direction index)

parameters.name: d_{e,e'}
parameters.meaning: planned running-time gap between event e and its successor (AfterEvent[0])

decision_variables.name: delta_e
decision_variables.meaning: 1 if a rolling-stock unit is held/present at the station of event e (rolling-stock balance indicator)

decision_variables.name: m_{e,e'}^{d}
decision_variables.meaning: 1 if in-event e (arriving) at depot d is connected to out-event e' (departing) at depot d via a turnaround

decision_variables.name: in_{e}^{d}
decision_variables.meaning: declared in-event selection variable for depot d (declared but no constraints/objective terms reference it in the released code)

decision_variables.name: out_{e}^{d}
decision_variables.meaning: declared out-event selection variable for depot d (declared but no constraints/objective terms reference it in the released code)

objective.expression_plain: minimize total cost of event cancellations (weighted by c_cancel) plus realized event times (weighted by c_delay). A constant additionobj = sum of c_delay * a_e over cancelled events is subtracted afterwards in post-processing to report net delay.

constraints.name: delay_lower_bound_active_event
constraints.expression_plain: for a non-cancelled event (z_e=0) the realized time must be at least its planned time; if cancelled (z_e=1) the realized time is pushed up by big-M. (c2 lower part)

constraints.name: delay_upper_bound
constraints.expression_plain: realized time of any event may not exceed its planned time by more than the horizon span M_dur. (c2 upper part)

constraints.name: precedence_running_time
constraints.indexed_over: e in E with successor e'=e.AfterEvent[0], for non-terminal stations, where planned gap > 0
constraints.expression_plain: the realized time of the successor event e' (plus a slack epsilon) must respect the realized time of e plus the planned running/dwell time between them (segment running-time / precedence constraint). (c3)

constraints.name: rolling_stock_count_per_station
constraints.expression_plain: the number of rolling-stock-holding indicators delta_e summed over events at station i (or its mirrored down-direction index) equals the prescribed train count n_i for that station. (CE / fleet balance)

constraints.name: event_continuity_interior
constraints.indexed_over: e in E whose former event has a valid arrival station (FormerEvent[0].ArrivalStation >= 0)
constraints.expression_plain: rolling-stock / event linking for an interior event: relates the cancellation of event e, the non-cancellation of its predecessor f(e), and the holding indicator delta_e. (continuity / flow conservation)

constraints.name: event_continuity_depot_boundary
constraints.indexed_over: e in E whose former event has no valid station (depot-boundary in/out events at station = |Stations| or 0)
constraints.expression_plain: for an out-event at a depot, the event is either cancelled, supplied by exactly one depot turnaround connection from an in-event, or held; the sum of these is at most one. (depot boundary continuity)

constraints.name: depot_out_connection_balance
constraints.indexed_over: d in {0,1}, out-event e in E^{out}_d at the depot-side station
constraints.expression_plain: each depot out-event is either cancelled or connected to exactly one in-event via a turnaround (train-circulation balance, out side).

constraints.name: depot_in_connection_balance
constraints.indexed_over: d in {0,1}, in-event e in E^{in}_d at the line-end station (with time window e.ArrivalTime < CurrentTime+14400)
constraints.expression_plain: each depot in-event is either cancelled or connected to exactly one out-event via a turnaround (train-circulation balance, in side).

constraints.name: turnaround_timing
constraints.indexed_over: d in {0,1}, in-event e_in in E^{in}_d, out-event e_out in E^{out}_d with a_{e_in} < a_{e_out}
constraints.expression_latex: M^{big} z_{e_{in}} + 2h\,(1 - m^{d}_{e_{in},e_{out}}) + t_{e_{out}} - t_{e_{in}} \geq T^{turn}\, m^{d}_{e_{in},e_{out}}
constraints.expression_plain: if in-event e_in is connected to out-event e_out at depot d (m=1) and e_in is not cancelled, the realized time gap between them must be at least the turnaround time T_turn; big-M and headway terms relax the constraint otherwise. (turnaround timing)

constraints.name: infeasible_turnaround_forbidden
constraints.indexed_over: d in {0,1}, in-event e_in, out-event e_out with a_{e_in} >= a_{e_out}
constraints.expression_plain: a turnaround connection from an in-event to an earlier-or-equal out-event is forbidden (chronological feasibility).

instances.name: I_1..I_10.csv
instances.size: 10 small-scale instances, ~16 KB each (e.g. I_1.csv 15910 bytes); planned timetable, one row = arrival+departure event

instances.name: I_6..I_25.csv
instances.size: 10 medium-scale instances, ~38 KB each (e.g. I_6.csv 37896 bytes)

instances.name: I_11..I_30.csv
instances.size: 10 large-scale instances, ~58 KB each (e.g. I_11.csv 58376 bytes)

instances.name: Section_running_time.txt / Section_distance.txt / Station_information.txt
instances.size: layout data for the 23-station Beijing metro line 1 (running times, distances, station info); 73-213 bytes

models.notes: 30 instances total (10 small / 10 medium / 10 large). Instance CSV headers and station names are GBK/Chinese-encoded. CancelPenalty=100000 and DelayPenalty=0 in the released InputParameters.cs mean the shipped objective minimizes cancellations only; DelayPenalty is varied for the weight tests (Output_Results/weight_test.xlsx). Variables ine/oute are declared but never appear in any constraint or the objective in the released code (the depot in/out balance is realized through mee, z, delta), so they are effectively unused. Constraint indices (c2, c3, CE, c4...) are taken from inline code comments; the c4 headway-time block is commented as a header but the only headway-using formulation in the released code is in the path-based MasterProblem (model 2). Big-M values are hard-coded (DurationM data-dependent; bigM=1e9). The objective realized-time term and the post-solve 'additionobj' subtraction together recover net delay relative to planned times.

models.description: Path-based RSTO (PRST) restricted master problem of the branch-and-price (BAP) scheme. Termed 'BAP' in the paper. Each candidate train path (a sequence of events) is a column; the master selects a fractional/integral combination of paths to cover the existing trains and the events while respecting headway and depot constraints, minimizing total path cost. Columns are generated by a shortest-path pricing subproblem on a space-time network (SPNetwork); this MasterProblem object solves the LP relaxation (NumVarType.Float) and returns duals for pricing. Integrality is enforced externally via the BCTree branch-and-bound (BCTree.cs).

sets_indices.name: P
sets_indices.meaning: set of all candidate paths (columns): existing-train paths plus depot-generated paths

sets_indices.name: K
sets_indices.meaning: set of existing trains (initial train positions)

sets_indices.name: P_k
sets_indices.meaning: subset of paths assignable to existing train k

sets_indices.name: E
sets_indices.meaning: set of events to be covered

sets_indices.name: Pr
sets_indices.meaning: set of event pairs used for headway constraints

parameters.name: c_p
parameters.meaning: cost of path p (cancellation/delay cost of the events it serves)

parameters.name: a_{ep}
parameters.meaning: 1 if path p covers event e (event-path incidence)

parameters.name: tau_{ep}
parameters.meaning: realized event-time contribution of path p at event e (PathEventTime)

parameters.name: g_{dp}
parameters.meaning: 1 if path p ends at depot d

parameters.name: N_d
parameters.meaning: required number of trains ending at depot d

decision_variables.name: x_p
decision_variables.meaning: selection weight of path p (LP relaxation of the binary path-selection variable; integrality enforced by branch-and-bound)

objective.expression_plain: minimize total cost of selected paths (a constant offset objectivevalue2 is added in post-processing to convert to the reported objective).

constraints.name: train_cover
constraints.expression_plain: exactly one path is selected for each existing train k.

constraints.name: event_set_cover
constraints.expression_plain: each event is covered by at most one selected path (set-packing). NOTE: the incidence term is commented out in the released code so the constraint is built as 0 <= 1; duals are still read for pricing.

constraints.name: headway
constraints.expression_plain: for each event pair, the headway between the current and former event implied by the selected paths must respect the minimum headway h (rearranged into a single >= row with constant 'barte').

constraints.name: depot_balance
constraints.expression_plain: the number of selected paths ending at depot d equals the required count N_d. NOTE: this AddEq call is commented out in the released code (the expression is built but not added to the model).

models.notes: This is the restricted master of a branch-and-price method; columns (paths) are priced via a shortest-path subproblem over the space-time network (SPNetwork, Method.cs:193-346) using duals DualTau/DualPi/DualPhi (Method.cs:523-526). The H-strategy initial solution comes from InitialSolution()/BCTree CurrentBestSolution. Two model rows in the released master (event_set_cover incidence term, and the depot_balance AddEq) are commented out in the shipped code; they are reported here as the intended formulation with that caveat flagged. Integrality of x_p is handled by BCTree.cs branch-and-bound (no CPLEX MILP model there), so this CPLEX object solves only the LP relaxation.

extraction_notes: Both formulations were read directly from CPLEX Concert .NET calls in src/WindowsFormsApplication7/Algorithm/Method.cs: EventBasedFormulation() (ERST/MIP, lines 711-1010) and MasterProblem() (PRST/BAP master, lines 427-527). Math symbols (z, t, delta, m, x) were assigned by me to match the code identifiers (recorded in source_symbol); the repo ships NO written mathematical formulation (README/ReadMe.md only describe replication steps, not equations), so symbol names and the c2/c3/CE constraint groupings come from terse inline code comments plus the variable semantics, not from a published notation in the repo. The associated paper (Yin, Yang, Liang, D'Ariano, Gao, INFORMS J. on Computing, DOI 10.1287/ijoc.2023.0391) presumably contains the canonical formulation but is not included in the archive, so confidence is MEDIUM rather than HIGH. Honest caveats: (1) variables ine/oute in model 1 are declared but unused; (2) in model 2, the event-set-cover incidence term and the depot_balance constraint are commented out in the released code (flagged in each constraint's note); (3) headway 'c4' is mentioned as a comment header in model 1 but not implemented there. Parameter values (CancelPenalty=100000, DelayPenalty=0, headway=60, turnaround=150) are the literal shipped defaults from InputParameters.cs and are varied across experiments. No DOIs, constraints, or symbols were fabricated; uncertain items are marked in notes rather than invented. BCTree.cs and Form1.cs also reference Cplex but contain no additional distinct LP/MILP model (branch-and-bound bookkeeping / GUI).
